Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all Swift Relocations vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true). Discovers three Swift bills all dated April 3, before the April 11 rate change: SR-2026-0419 ($2,885 for Kevin Tran, Portland to Denver, Vectral Systems, breakdown: $2,800 base and $85 bike handling), SR-2026-0421 ($4,450 for Priya Venkatesh, Houston to Seattle, GreenStack Energy, breakdown: $3,800 base and $650 electronics surcharge), and SR-2026-0422 ($3,675 for Ines Kovac, Brooklyn to Austin, PivotPoint Labs, breakdown: $3,200 base and $350 walkup and $125 electronics). No post April 11 Swift vendor bills exist yet in QuickBooks, which means the agent cannot calculate exact margin impact for later moves and must note this data gap.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for April client invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Discovers the client facing invoices to compare against vendor bills: INV-2026-0410 (Vectral Systems $15,900 for Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya), INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack Energy $9,440 for Zhao and Reyes), INV-2026-0412 (NorthWind Technologies $14,700 for Patel and Cruz), INV-2026-0401 (Canopy Health $9,210 for Kovacs, Webb, Okonkwo), INV-2026-0315 (PivotPoint Labs $10,650 for Okonjo and Castañeda), INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic Robotics $9,010 for Okoye, Hartley, Tanaka), INV-2026-0308 (BrightLoop Analytics $11,350 for Richter and Webb). Also discovers EST-2026-0423 (Terraform Digital $6,560 estimate dated April 23 for 3 relocations: Dasgupta, Liu, Obi).
OE 3: Search Airtable for relocation records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to identify which April relocations used Swift as the moving vendor. Vendor assignments are embedded in the Special Requirements text field, not a dedicated vendor column. Discovers relocations explicitly mentioning Swift including: Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, Columbus to Seattle, hazmat laser cutter, "Swift crew" and "Swift MUST sign"), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, Austin to SF, split shipment with Heartland and Swift for Shipment 2, ~3,200 lbs), Dr. Renata Kovacs (Canopy, Nashville to Toronto, "Swift Relocations handling full move"), Marcus Webb (Canopy, Detroit to Chicago, Swift handling cold chain freight for cryogenic containers), and Raj Patel (NorthWind, Chicago to Denver, "confirm Swift will accept supervised loading protocol"). Additional Swift assignments are discoverable through cross-service investigation: Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, Houston to Portland, Airtable says "certified hazmat carrier" but CRM engagement confirms "VENDOR: Swift Relocations assigned"), Priya Suresh (Vectral, Portland to Denver, CRM engagement confirms "Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations"), Simone Richter and Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, CRM deal text confirms Swift Relocations). The QuickBooks vendor bills from OE 1 also confirm Swift handled Kevin Tran (Vectral), Priya Venkatesh (GreenStack), and Ines Kovac (PivotPoint). Cross-referencing these with the invoices from OE 2 enables the client by client margin comparison.
OE 4: Search emails for Greg Pallone's pricing change notification using search_emails (query: "Pricing Update" or "Swift rate increase" or "Greg Pallone" or "DOT surcharge"). Discovers the email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject line "Pricing Update Effective Immediately DOT Documentation Surcharge" confirming: 15% base rate increase on all new bookings effective April 11, 2026, and $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment where Swift crews manage added compliance paperwork.
OE 5: Search emails for Hana's GreenStack invoice correspondence using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0418" or "GreenStack hazmat" or "hazmat surcharge"). Discovers Hana's email to Marcus about INV-2026-0418 ($9,440) flagging: hazmat transport surcharge set at $1,800 for DOT Class 9 lithium ion battery modules (Mei-Lin Zhao), with Hana stating she lacks a comparable invoice for reference and asking Marcus to verify the rate. Also finds the $1,650 interim hazmat storage surcharge (11 day gap, April 17 to 28) noted as pending client approval from Derek Hollis, and the $950 overweight equipment surcharge for Joaquín Reyes's 900 lb solar rig.
OE 6: Search Slack for Hana's NorthWind invoice discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "INV-2026-0412" or "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "piano"). Discovers Hana's message in the finance channel (C005) about INV-2026-0412 ($14,700) flagging two provisional line items: chain of custody security protocol at $1,200 (described as "PROVISIONAL" because "MoveOps has literally never offered this service before," priced from industry benchmarks), and specialty piano moving at $2,200 (Steinway Model B, 480 lbs, includes Denver tuning). Also flags early lease termination negotiation service at $500 for Emilia Cruz as potentially outside standard MoveOps scope.
OE 7: Search emails for Canopy batch surcharge details using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0401" or "customs broker" or "cold chain" or "Dr. Kovacs" or "Marcus Webb cryogenic"). Discovers the Canopy Q2 invoice line items: $850 TN visa/customs broker surcharge for Dr. Renata Kovacs (Nashville to Toronto, BorderEase Customs engagement), $1,200 cold chain specialized freight surcharge for Marcus Webb (Detroit to Chicago, split vendor arrangement with Swift handling cold chain freight for cryogenic containers and Heartland handling household goods), and $600 temporary storage fee for Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia to Denver, 2 week lease gap).
OE 8: Search emails for Vectral surcharge planning using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0410" or "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Vectral April relocations"). Discovers the Vectral April invoice line items: $350 pet relocation coordination surcharge for Priya Suresh (emotional support German Shepherd named Atlas, 85 lbs, ESA documentation, requires pet friendly housing with fenced yard in RiNo or Highlands), $400 ADA housing sourcing premium for Terrence Nakamura (wheelchair user requiring roll in shower, 36 inch doorways, ground floor or elevator, SoMa or Mission Bay preferred), and $900 split shipment surcharge for Nakamura (9,200 lbs total, Heartland ~6,000 lbs Shipment 1, Swift ~3,200 lbs Shipment 2).
OE 9: Search emails for Terraform scope details using search_emails (query: "Rachel Nguyen" or "EST-2026-0423" or "Terraform relocations"). Discovers Rachel Nguyen's email listing 3 relocations: Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, target move in May 5), Marcus Liu (Portland to NYC, May 12), Samantha Obi (Denver to SF, May 8). Also search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "EST-2026-0423" or "volume discount") to find Marcus's reply in the finance channel (C005) warning not to convert the estimate to invoice until the volume discount structure is finalized, and noting Marcus Liu's 2 BR equivalent with specialty packing may exceed the $500 moving coordination estimate.
OE 10: Search Linear for the vendor margin dataset ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor margin dataset"). Discovers issue linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (issue 214), titled "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM," assigned to Alejandro Fuentes (moveops_alejandro_fuentes), priority P1, due April 16. Description references both the Heartland Q3 rate adjustment and Swift's immediate pricing changes.
OE 11: Search Slack for finance channel discussions about Swift pricing and vendor costs using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Swift" or "15% increase" or "vendor margin" or "margin exposure"). Discovers Alejandro's message in C005 about data quality problems preventing a defensible client by client margin forecast (Airtable vendor field missing, QuickBooks bills not tagged to relocation records, CRM holds deal level data without vendor cost linkage). Also discovers Elena's vendor strategy directive in C004 demanding the memo by Wednesday (April 29).
OE 12: Search CRM for affected client company records and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") and crm_search_deals (company_id: company_vectral), repeated for GreenStack, Canopy, NorthWind, PivotPoint, Mosaic, and BrightLoop. Discovers company IDs and active deal records for each client. These company IDs are needed to create CRM engagement notes in OE 18.
OE 13: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Confirms marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The agent may also look up Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com) and Alejandro Fuentes (alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) for the Slack post.
OE 14: Post margin impact analysis in the finance channel for Alejandro using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, payload: margin analysis summary). The message should include the client by client breakdown of Swift margin exposure, which invoices are affected, which accounts are at risk, data quality gaps found, and recommended actions before the vendor memo goes to Elena.
OE 15: Update the vendor margin dataset ticket with Swift cost exposure findings using linear_create_comment (issueId: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170, body: Swift cost analysis data) or linear_update_issue. The comment should include the Swift April relocation cost data, identified data gaps (no post April 11 vendor bills in QuickBooks), and recommendations for the Q3 margin model.
OE 16: Send Marcus a summary email using send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com]). The email should summarize which client accounts are affected by the Swift pricing change, whether any invoices need revision before the vendor memo goes to Elena, the specific invoice line item discrepancies found during verification (GreenStack hazmat, NorthWind chain of custody and piano, Canopy customs and cold chain, Vectral ADA and pet), and the Terraform estimate readiness status.
OE 17: Block calendar time with Marcus for April 28 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: margin review or similar, start_datetime: 2026-04-28, attendees: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com]). Purpose is to walk through the numbers before the April 29 vendor memo deadline.
OE 18: Add CRM engagement notes for affected client accounts using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, body: margin exposure alert with client specific details, company_ids: [relevant company ID]). The prompt explicitly asks to "check the CRM deals for each affected client and add a note about the margin exposure so the account managers know before their next client conversation." Should create engagement notes for clients with confirmed Swift related April moves: Vectral Systems (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura via CRM engagement; Kevin Tran via vendor bill), GreenStack Energy (Mei-Lin Zhao via CRM engagement; Priya Venkatesh via vendor bill), Canopy Health (Dr. Renata Kovacs and Marcus Webb via Airtable and CRM), NorthWind Technologies (Raj Patel via Airtable and CRM deal), PivotPoint Labs (Ines Kovac via vendor bill), Mosaic Robotics (Keiko Tanaka via Airtable), and BrightLoop Analytics (Simone Richter and Marcus Webb via CRM deal).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all Swift Relocations vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true).
